pattern: Map each word to the integer formed by concatenating the alphabet positions of its letters, then test whether the first two sums equal the third.
hints: Each letter contributes a digit equal to its distance from the letter a.
Build the number for a word by appending each letter's digit, which is the same as treating the word as a base-ten number of those digits.
Convert all three words, then return whether the first value plus the second equals the third.

edge_inputs.description: leading-zero words still sum to zero but target differs
max-length words from a to j

stress.description: random short words over a to j
